free_text: The reaction was studied up to 3.5 MeV
using the Rice University Van de Graaff and
Up to 4.8 MeV Using the Machine at Saclay
The Rice experiments were done with natural lithium
target of about 20 ug/cm2 deposited on 50 ug/cm2 carbon
backing. The target used in the Saclay experiments was
about 10 to 15 ug/cm2 deposited on carbon foils
The excitation curves at eight angles were obtained
using solid state counters as detectors of alpha
particles. The target deterioration was checked after
each excitation curve and was found to be less than 5%.
In the Saclay experiments: the angular distribution at
each energy was studied from 90 to 170 Degr. using a
solid state detector. A solid state detector at 90 degr
Uncertainty due to monitor reaction and
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
The angular distributions obtained at Saclay
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
SD: SF8=REL added; SF9=EXP deleted from
DATA-ERR1 -> ERR-S. Data units were changed
The coefficients A and B as a functions of energy
En and number columns are changed.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
The error bars include also the errors quoted in the
references for the energies 1.47 (Freeman) at which
